title: picraena antillana
intro: Picraena antillana, also known as Picrasma antillana, is a plant species that belongs to the family Simaroubaceae. It is native to the Caribbean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Picraena antillana include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and its bark is sometimes used in traditional medicine to make remedies for fever and digestive issues.
